facts: A lever uses a long stick that rocks on a point called the fulcrum; pushing down on one end lifts the other end, helping you move a heavy object with less effort.
A wheel and axle work together like on a bicycle; turning the big wheel makes the small axle turn, which helps move you forward.
A ramp is a slanted surface that lets you roll a heavy object up instead of lifting it straight up; the longer the ramp, the less force you need to push.
A pulley is a wheel with a rope around it; pulling down on the rope lifts the load up, so you can use your weight to help lift.
misconceptions: A longer ramp makes it harder to push an object up because you have to push a longer distance; in fact, a longer ramp reduces the force needed to lift the object.
A pulley makes the load lighter because you pull down instead of up; actually, a fixed pulley only changes the direction of your pull, not the amount of force needed.
